admissionRules: Every reference used by a P10 binding must resolve to exactly one candidate registry entry.
Candidate reference admission records provenance and consumers but does not assert external source approval or production readiness.
Context-family and numeric-profile entries must preserve PatternSpec ownership.
Semantic-variant and language-variant entries must preserve one-to-one binding ownership.
Common-knowledge and answer-unit entries may be shared across bindings but must list all consumers deterministically.
All candidate references remain lifecycleStatus candidate and approvalState candidate.
Formal approved semantic bindings remain empty.
blockingRules: Block on any unresolved or multiply-resolved cross-registry reference.
Block on duplicate candidate registry IDs or duplicate review packet IDs.
Block if candidate provenance or consumer ownership is empty.
Block if a review packet omits semantic or mathematical checks.
Block if any review packet contains an automatic approve/reject decision.
Block if any candidate or binding claims approved lifecycle, production selection or runtime resolution.
Block if the formal approved registry contains any entry.

distance.distanceReduced: all candidate binding references become resolvable and 117 deterministic human review packets are created without false approval
